mobilityBills.title: Shaping the Future
mobilityBills.facilitiesDescription: 10 Bengaluru metro stations and 11 BMTC, TTMC stations have a basic provision of safe cycle parking and an integrated bicycle repair kiosk close to the station premises, sometimes inside the paid parking area. The metro stations also have walking facilities which work well in some and not so well in others. The goal is to increase the first mile/last mile to the metro and demand better cycling & walking facilities be extended to all metro stations.
mobilityBills.planningDescription: The 2020 Comprehensive Mobility Plan finalised by the Directorate of Urban Land Transport along with BMRCL has planned for 974 kms of footpath to be constructed with 600 kms of cycle track and 103 kms of Tender Sure Roads. In order to make these plans implementable, we will use the challenge as an opportunity to drive these conversations further.
mobilityBills.policyDescription: The Active Mobility Bill, drafted by DULT (Urban Development Department), provides a framework for Karnataka cities—especially those above 50,000 population—to promote walking and cycling. It proposes dedicated active mobility cells to enable budgeting, infrastructure delivery, and maintenance, and includes citizen audits and grievance redressal mechanisms.
mobilityBills.currentInfrastructureTitle: Here's the infrastructure we have currently
mobilityBills.futurePlansTitle: Here's what we're looking to bolster

whyHejjeGala.supportShiftTitle: Support Bengaluru's shift to sustainable mobility

faqs.question: What is Hejje Gala?
faqs.answer: Hejje Gala is a city-wide corporate challenge in Bengaluru that encourages employees to walk and cycle as part of their daily commute. Organisations officially register and motivate their teams to choose healthier, more sustainable ways of getting to work.

faqs.question: What does "active mobility" mean?
faqs.answer: Active mobility refers to walking and cycling - either as your primary way of commuting or as a first/last-mile connection to public transport like metro or bus.

faqs.question: Who is behind Hejje Gala?
faqs.answer: Hejje Gala is organised by Urban Morph in partnership with the Greater Bengaluru Authority (GBA), WRI India, Walkaluru (coordinated by Arun Pai, who has led a walking revolution in Bengaluru), and the Bicycle Mayor of Bengaluru. Together, these partners bring expertise in urban mobility, infrastructure policy, and grassroots advocacy.

faqs.question: Who can participate?
faqs.answer: Hejje Gala is open to everyone in Bengaluru — individuals, organisations, cycling clubs, walking groups, and communities. You can participate by joining community events, becoming an ambassador for Altmo, or tracking your active commutes via Altmo.

faqs.question: Can organisations outside Bengaluru participate?
faqs.answer: No - Hejje Gala is specifically designed for Bengaluru. Your office location and employees' commutes should be within the city.

faqs.question: Is there a minimum number of employees required?
faqs.answer: No minimum is required. Organisations of all sizes are welcome - from startups to large enterprises. What matters is participation and commitment, not headcount.

faqs.question: Is there a registration fee?
faqs.answer: No. Participation in Hejje Gala is free for all organisations.

faqs.question: Can employees from multiple office locations within the same organisation participate?
faqs.answer: Yes. If your organisation has multiple offices in Bengaluru, employees from all locations can participate under a single organisational registration.

faqs.question: How can I help drive participation at my company?
faqs.answer: You can ask your company representatives to sign up at [altmo.app/corporates](https://altmo.app/users/sign_up?corporates=true&plan_name=corporate&verification=pending). You can also refer your colleagues to download the altmo mobile app and join the challenge.

faqs.question: How do organisations register?
faqs.answer: Individuals in companies can just download the altmo mobile app from [Play Store](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=app.altmo) / [App Store](https://apps.apple.com/app/altmo/id1554339886) or sign up at [www.altmo.app](https://www.altmo.app). Find the Hejje Gala challenge in the challenges tab and click join. Organisation representatives can click the Get Started button at [altmo.app/corporates](https://altmo.app/users/sign_up?corporates=true&plan_name=corporate&verification=pending) — we will get into a discussion with them on getting access to the backend dashboard for their employees.

faqs.question: What support does Hejje Gala provide to participating organisations?
faqs.answer: Registered organisations receive support to plan and promote their events. You can submit community events through the [Activities page](/activities), which appear on the shared community calendar.

faqs.question: How is participation tracked?
faqs.answer: Corporate challenge participation is tracked through Altmo. Community events (walks, rides, workshops) are listed on the community calendar at [hejjegala.in/activities](https://hejjegala.in/activities), where anyone can submit and discover events that you can participate in.

faqs.question: Do I have to use Altmo?
faqs.answer: Yes - the challenge relies on Altmo to track and verify activities. Altmo has a built-in tracker, so you can use it directly on your phone. If you're more comfortable using another device (like a smartwatch or fitness tracker), you can either sync it with Strava or upload your .gpx file on the tracker page of the website [altmo.app](https://altmo.app) after you login. If you are using Strava, you will need to connect your Strava account from your profile page on the website. Make sure you've selected the correct activity type (walking or cycling) - only then will your activities count on the leaderboard.

faqs.question: What counts as a valid commute? Do weekend rides count?
faqs.answer: For the corporate challenge, only commutes to your workplace count towards your organisation's total. Community events (walks, rides, workshops) are separate — anyone can join these regardless of corporate affiliation.

faqs.question: What happens if I work from home some days?
faqs.answer: That's fine - you only log activity on days you commute. There's no penalty for working from home. Every active commute you make during the challenge period counts toward your organisation's total.

faqs.question: What data is measured?
faqs.textAfter: All data is presented at an aggregated level for organisations.

faqs.question: How does Altmo protect my privacy?
faqs.answer: Altmo has a strong privacy policy vetted by multinational corporations with world-class privacy standards. Personal data is never shared or sold. Only de-identified route information is provided to government agencies to help improve infrastructure.

faqs.question: Are there prizes or recognition?
faqs.answer: Yes. Organisations demonstrating strong participation and impact will be publicly recognised for their leadership in promoting active mobility and sustainable commuting.

faqs.question: How are winners determined?
faqs.answer:
Winners are organisations, not individuals. Hejje Gala recognises companies based on a combination of factors including participation rate (percentage of employees who took part), total kilometres logged, and consistency throughout the challenge period. This ensures organisations of all sizes have a fair chance at recognition.

As the saying goes, "If you want to go fast, go alone. If you want to go far, go together." Hejje Gala is built on the belief that collective action benefits everyone - when organisations commit together, we create the momentum needed to transform how Bengaluru moves.

That said, companies are free to create their own internal challenges on Altmo and reward their employees as they see fit. We can walk your company representative through how to set this up.

faqs.question: Is there a leaderboard?
faqs.answer: Yes. Participating organisations can view a leaderboard showing how they compare to others. This adds a friendly competitive element and helps motivate teams throughout the challenge. The leaderboard tracks corporate participation. Community events are visible on the [Activities page](/activities) where you can browse upcoming walks, rides, and workshops.

faqs.question: What is Altmo's track record?
faqs.answer: Altmo has already shaped real infrastructure decisions in Bengaluru. Data from Altmo and the Bicycle Mayor influenced the allocation of budgets for cycle tracks and footpaths in the Comprehensive Mobility Plan of the Government of Karnataka, 2020. Altmo data also informed the planning of cycle lanes on the Outer Ring Road. Altmo was the official technology platform for the Smart City Mission's "Freedom to Walk, Cycle and Run" challenge, where officials from 30 cities across India competed on an Altmo-powered leaderboard.

faqs.question: What has Walkaluru achieved?
faqs.answer: Walkaluru has driven tangible change on the ground. Through sustained advocacy and community action, GBA and corporations have improved many kilometres of footpaths across Bengaluru, making the city more walkable for everyone. Over 1,500 citizens have walked over 100 kilometres on footpaths across 12 public events since November 2025 - as part of a mission to make Bengaluru a Walkaluru.

faqs.question: What has WRI India contributed?
faqs.answer: WRI India has transformed numerous junctions through their Suraksha 75 improvement plan, making streets safer for pedestrians and cyclists. They have led multiple successful campaigns promoting public transport and have been instrumental in shaping Bengaluru's Climate Action Plan and the Transit Oriented Development policy.

faqs.question: Will Hejje Gala lead to better infrastructure?
faqs.answer: Yes. The partners behind Hejje Gala have a proven track record of turning data and advocacy into real change. With corporate participation generating new commute data and demonstrating demand for active mobility, we aim to accelerate infrastructure improvements and policy reforms that make walking and cycling safer and more accessible across Bengaluru.

faqs.question: How do I find upcoming community events?
faqs.answer: Visit the [Activities page](https://hejjegala.in/activities). You can browse events in a calendar view or card view, and filter by type — Walk, Cycle, or Other.

faqs.question: How do I add a community event?
faqs.answer: Click the "Add Event" button on the [Activities page](/activities). Fill in the event details — title, type, date, time, venue, and description. You can optionally add an RSVP link, Google Maps link, and event poster. Events appear on the calendar immediately.

faqs.question: What types of events can I submit?
faqs.answer: You can submit walks, cycling events, or other active mobility activities like street audits, walkability workshops, or community rides. Events should be related to walking, cycling, or sustainable mobility in Bengaluru.

faqs.question: Do I need an account to submit an event?
faqs.answer: No. Anyone can submit a community event without logging in. We trust the community to keep the calendar useful and relevant.

faqs.question: Can I edit or remove my event after submitting?
faqs.answer: Currently, events cannot be edited after submission. If you need to update or remove an event, contact us at [hejjegala@gmail.com](mailto:hejjegala@gmail.com).

faqs.question: Who can I contact for more information?
faqs.answer: Email [hejjegala@gmail.com](mailto:hejjegala@gmail.com) for questions about participation, partnerships, or the challenge format.
